description: Organize rooms into hierarchical trees called spaces. Browse and navigate nested room structures with paginated breadth-first traversal and federated child resolution.

actors.name: Space Administrator
actors.description: User with power to manage child relationships in a space

actors.name: User
actors.description: User browsing or navigating the space hierarchy

actors.name: Homeserver
actors.description: Server traversing and caching space hierarchy data

actors.name: Remote Server
actors.description: Foreign server hosting child rooms or sub-spaces

fields.name: space_room_id
fields.label: Room ID of the space (a room with type m.space)

fields.name: child_room_id
fields.label: Room ID of a child within the space

fields.name: via_servers
fields.label: Server names to contact when resolving the child room

fields.name: suggested
fields.label: Whether the child is highlighted as a recommended entry point

fields.name: order
fields.label: Optional lexicographic ordering hint for display

fields.name: max_depth
fields.label: Maximum traversal depth; null means unlimited

fields.name: pagination_token
fields.label: Opaque token referencing a paginated traversal session

fields.name: suggested_only
fields.label: If true, only suggested children are included in results

values.description: Pagination session is valid and may be resumed
Session has not been accessed within 5 minutes and is invalidated

transitions.description: Session idle timeout reached after 5 minutes

structure: A space is a room whose creation event specifies the space room type
Child relationships are defined by state events in the parent space room; each child room ID is the state key
A child state event must include a list of servers to contact for resolution
traversal: Traversal is breadth-first; oldest forward extremities in the queue are processed first
A maximum of 50 rooms may be returned per page
A maximum of 50 children per space are processed in a single traversal step
At most 3 servers are queried when resolving a remote child room
Pagination sessions expire after 5 minutes of inactivity
Maximum traversal depth is configurable; depth 0 returns only the root space
access: Regular users only see rooms they are permitted to access; server administrators see all rooms
Remote child rooms are fetched via federation hierarchy API

any: requester is a member of the space

given: pagination session is valid or no token provided

then.description: Pagination session created or resumed with traversal state

hierarchy_page_returned.result: Batch of rooms from the space hierarchy returned with optional continuation token

given: all reachable rooms within max_depth have been visited
hierarchy_traversal_complete.result: Final page returned with no continuation token

given: child room is hosted on a remote server
at least one via_server is reachable
remote_child_resolved.result: Remote child room details included in hierarchy response

given: child room is hosted on a remote server
remote_child_unresolvable.result: Child room omitted from results; traversal continues

given: requester is not a member of the space
requester is not a server administrator
pagination token references a session that has expired
session_expired.result: Client must restart traversal from the beginning

errors.message: You do not have permission to browse this space
Pagination session has expired. Please restart the request.
The requested space room was not found

events.name: space.hierarchy.page_returned
events.description: A page of rooms from the space hierarchy was returned to the requester

events.name: space.hierarchy.complete
events.description: All reachable rooms within the traversal depth have been visited

events.name: space.child.resolved
events.description: A remote child room was successfully resolved via federation

events.name: space.child.unresolvable
events.description: A child room could not be reached from any listed server

related.reason: Spaces are rooms; they are created through the same room creation flow
Child relationships are stored as state events in the space room
Only users with sufficient power may manage child state events
Remote child rooms are resolved via federated hierarchy queries
